//: The `collect` step assumes the production build already exists on
disk (we don't run `next build` here to keep CI steps orthogonal —
the build job runs once, the lhci job starts the already-built
server). Locally: `npm run build && npx lhci autorun`.
`assertMatrix` lets us tune thresholds per URL. The auth page
intentionally carries `robots: noindex` (we do NOT want sign-in
flows showing up in Google), which crashes Lighthouse's SEO score
to ~0.58 — so we drop the SEO category for anything under /auth.
* a11y / best-practices — strict. Regressions here are bugs.
* SEO — strict on public pages, off on noindex pages.
* performance — lenient and `warn`-level. The MapTiler SDK alone
is ~400KB gzipped and there's no painless way around that on
the map page. Tightening this is a future perf work item;
today we just want a guard-rail against catastrophic regressions.
